sections.paragraph:
with suff. אֲשָׁמֹו Pl. with suff. אֲשָׁמָיו fault, guilt, blame, which any one incurs, Gen 26:10 Jer 51:5. Hence-

(1) that by which any one contracts guilt, Num 5:7, .

(2) sacrifice for transgression, 1Sa 6:3, seq.; 2Ki 12:17 Isa 53:10 Eze 40:39. In the Mosaic law there is a careful distinction between these sacrifices for trespass (אֲשָׁמִים), and sacrifices for sin (חַטָּאֹות). Not only were the ceremonies used in the two cases different (see -Lev. 5:126 , Engl. Ver. -119 ; and -6:17 ; -7:17 ; compare -4:135 ; -6:1723 , Engl. Ver. -2430 ), but in one and the same offering both kinds of victims were sometimes joined (as Lev 14:10, seq.; Num 6:12, seq.; comp. -Lev. 5:710 ); and the particular faults or sins which were to be expiated by the one or the other offering are carefully laid down in the law (see Levit. 5:15 -12:24 ; -19:2022 ; Num 6:11, 12 ); although the exact difference between each kind of sin has hitherto been vainly inquired[?]. See Joseph. Antiquit. iii. 9, § 3 Philo, De Victimis, ii. page 247; Mang., Rosenm. on Lev 5:6 Carpzov, Antiquit. S. Cod. page 707, seq.
